title: Turner Prize 2026
summary: Britain's most prestigious contemporary art award returns to Tate Britain with four shortlisted artists.
description:
# Turner Prize 2026

Now in its 42nd year, the Turner Prize remains the UK's most important contemporary art award, celebrating the originality and vitality of art in Britain today.

This year's shortlist spans painting, sculpture, installation and moving image — an exhibition that asks urgent questions about identity, ecology and the futures we build together.
heroImageAlt: Contemporary sculpture in a gallery space

title: David Hockney: Late Works
summary: An intimate survey of Hockney's luminous work from the last decade, including new iPad compositions.
description:
# David Hockney: Late Works

Spanning the past decade of Hockney's prolific practice, this exhibition brings together more than 80 paintings, drawings and digital works. The galleries chart a late flowering of colour and attention — a sustained looking at the ordinary miracles of light, landscape and company.
heroImageAlt: Vibrant abstract painting with swirling colours

title: Women in Revolt!
summary: Art, activism and the women's movement in the UK, 1970–1990.
description:
# Women in Revolt!

Drawing together more than 100 women artists, this landmark exhibition reveals the astonishing breadth of feminist artmaking in the UK between 1970 and 1990 — from photography and film to painting, performance and punk ephemera.
heroImageAlt: Archive posters and photographs on a gallery wall

title: Van Gogh: Poets and Lovers
summary: A landmark gathering of Van Gogh's masterpieces exploring his visions of Arles and Saint-Rémy.
description:
# Van Gogh: Poets and Lovers

Bringing together key works from Van Gogh's most fertile years, this exhibition follows his ambition to paint a modern art of feeling — the sunflowers, the starry nights, the letters to Theo. With more than 50 paintings and drawings, many rarely loaned.

title: Siena: The Rise of Painting, 1300–1350
summary: Five decades that transformed European art, told through gilded altarpieces and tender saints.
description:
# Siena: The Rise of Painting

In the early 14th century, a small group of artists working in Siena — Duccio, Simone Martini, the Lorenzetti brothers — reinvented what a painting could be. This exhibition gathers masterpieces from across Europe for the first time in a generation.

title: Fashioning Masculinities
summary: Four centuries of menswear on display, from ruffs to runways.
description:
# Fashioning Masculinities

Examining how clothes shape the way men — and those identifying as masculine — present themselves to the world, this exhibition draws on the V&A's encyclopaedic fashion collection, paired with paintings, photographs and film.

title: Gabrielle Chanel: Fashion Manifesto
summary: The first UK exhibition devoted to the revolutionary work of Gabrielle 'Coco' Chanel.
description:
# Gabrielle Chanel: Fashion Manifesto

Through nearly 200 looks spanning six decades, this show traces the evolution of Chanel's design vocabulary — the little black dress, the tweed suit, the No. 5 — and her radical idea that simplicity could be luxury.
heroImageAlt: A black and white gown displayed on a mannequin

title: Summer Exhibition 2026
summary: The world's oldest open-submission exhibition, held without interruption since 1769.
description:
# Summer Exhibition 2026

Over 1,000 works from both emerging and established artists, selected from thousands of open submissions. A chaotic, exhilarating snapshot of art being made in the UK right now.
heroImageAlt: Paintings densely hung salon-style on gallery walls

title: Ai Weiwei: Making Sense
summary: A major new survey examining the artist's obsession with objects, value and cultural meaning.
description:
# Ai Weiwei: Making Sense

Five vast 'fields' of objects — porcelain cannonballs, Stone Age tools, smashed Han dynasty jars — invite viewers to reflect on craft, destruction, heritage, and what we choose to preserve.
heroImageAlt: Large-scale installation of ceramic objects arranged on a gallery floor

title: Serpentine Pavilion 2026
summary: The 25th edition of the annual architectural commission in Kensington Gardens.
description:
# Serpentine Pavilion 2026

A 25-year tradition, the Pavilion is unveiled each summer as a living space for performance, dialogue and rest. This year's design by a leading international architect will be revealed in June.
heroImageAlt: Contemporary architectural pavilion in a London park

title: Anish Kapoor: Forms
summary: New sculpture by one of the most influential artists working today.
description:
# Anish Kapoor: Forms

This exhibition brings together recent sculpture exploring voids, pigment and pure geometry. Kapoor's objects are both objects and absences — they pull the gaze inward.
heroImageAlt: Minimalist sculpture with polished reflective surface

title: Mixing It Up: Painting Today
summary: Thirty-one painters working in Britain today, charting the medium's unruly vitality.
description:
# Mixing It Up: Painting Today

From figuration to abstraction, from intimate portraits to expansive landscapes, this exhibition asks what painting means now — in a moment saturated with images, where the painted mark still insists on time, touch, attention.
heroImageAlt: Contemporary painting with bold colours on gallery wall

title: Nicole Eisenman: What Happened
summary: The first UK survey of the American painter's restless, wickedly perceptive work.
description:
# Nicole Eisenman: What Happened

Eisenman's paintings and sculptures track a generation — its parties, protests, intimacies and anxieties — with tenderness, humour and an unerring eye for the absurd.

title: Flowers — Flora in Contemporary Art
summary: Thirty contemporary artists reimagine the botanical across painting, sculpture and moving image.
description:
# Flowers

A lush gathering of recent work that uses flora — as motif, metaphor and material — to think through ecology, memory, and the porous line between natural and cultural worlds.
heroImageAlt: Close-up of a contemporary painting featuring botanical imagery

title: Cézanne: Drawing
summary: Cézanne's drawings and watercolours — the private engine behind the paintings.
description:
# Cézanne: Drawing

Over 80 drawings and watercolours reveal how Cézanne thought on paper — sketching apples, Mont Sainte-Victoire, his own hand — working out the problems that would preoccupy modern art for a century.
heroImageAlt: Framed drawings hanging in a gallery

title: British Photography Now
summary: New work from twelve photographers mapping the textures of contemporary British life.
description:
# British Photography Now

Work across reportage, staged tableaux and documentary by twelve photographers shortlisted from an open call. The camera as a tool for slow attention in a fast country.
heroImageAlt: Photographic prints displayed on a gallery wall
